seoTitle: How to Merge Tickets in Zendesk to Remove Duplicates
metaDescription: Combine duplicate Zendesk tickets into a single record: find the master ticket, merge the secondary one into it, and manage visibility of the merge note.
intro: When a customer submits the same request twice, or two agents accidentally pick up the same issue, merging tickets keeps your support queue from getting cluttered. This guide covers finding the duplicate, choosing the surviving master ticket, and controlling whether the customer sees the merge notification.

steps.heading: Find the duplicate ticket
steps.body: Open the secondary ticket in Zendesk Support that you want to close out as a duplicate.

steps.heading: Open the merge dialog
steps.body: Click the three-dot options menu in the upper right and select "Merge into another ticket."

steps.heading: Choose the master ticket
steps.body: Search for and input the target ticket ID that should serve as the surviving record.

steps.heading: Review what transfers
steps.body: Check which ticket will close and confirm how the comment history will map into the master ticket.

steps.heading: Set comment visibility
steps.body: Toggle whether the requester sees the automatic merge notification comment.

steps.heading: Confirm the merge
steps.body: Click confirm to lock in the merge and update your active ticket views.

faq.question: Can I undo a ticket merge?
faq.answer: Merged tickets are generally not reversible, so double-check you've selected the correct master ticket before confirming.

faq.question: Does the customer see that their tickets were merged?
faq.answer: That depends on the visibility toggle you set during the merge, which controls whether the requester sees a notification comment.

faq.question: What happens to attachments on the ticket that closes?
faq.answer: The system carries over comment history during a merge, including attachments, into the surviving master ticket.

faq.question: How can I tell a ticket was closed by a merge later on?
faq.answer: Zendesk applies a background tag like closed_by_merge to help you identify merged tickets in reporting.
